articles.title: Brent crude slips as markets await clarity on US-Iran peace deal
articles.description: Brent crude oil prices dip as markets await details on the US-Iran peace deal, with mixed trading results.

articles.title: Stock Market Opening Bell: Sensex Opens Above 76,500, Nifty Near 23,900; Can Markets Climb Further?
articles.description: Stock Market Today | Opening Bell: The Indian share markets opened positive on June 16 due to lower oil prices and improved geopolitics. The Sensex rose by more than 300 points, while the Nifty approached the 24,000 mark.

articles.title: Most Stocks Rise, Oil Prices Flat Following Peace Deal-Fuelled Rally
articles.description: Oil industry watchers caution that market conditions will likely be tight for a period of weeks or months

articles.title: Asia Stocks Climb Amid U.S.-Iran Deal and BOJ Rate Hike
articles.description: Asian stocks made cautious gains amid U.S.-Iran peace deal news and a Bank of Japan rate hike. While the yen rose, the Nikkei 225 reached a new peak. Oil prices remained low amid lingering geopolitical uncertainties. Wall Street saw strong results, reflecting investor optimism despite potential future challenges.

articles.title: John Bolton Says Trump's Focus On Price Of Gas At The Pump Makes His Iran Strategy 'So Transparent' That
articles.description: John Bolton warns Trump's focus on gas prices hurts US position in talks with Iran. Analysts predict relief at pump may take months or years.

articles.title: Stock Market Today: Sensex Gains 300 Points, Nifty Tops 23,900 In Early Trade
articles.description: Indian benchmark indices open higher as BSE Sensex and Nifty 50 gain in early trade, supported by positive global cues and easing crude oil prices

articles.title: Sensex rises over 250 points, Nifty above 23,900 as markets extend gains; HCL Tech among top gainers
articles.description: Indian stock markets surged for a third consecutive day, with Sensex and Nifty extending gains amid optimism around a potential Iran-US peace deal framework. While positive macro factors like falling crude oil prices and a stable rupee offer resilience, concerns remain regarding the monsoon's impact on inflation and FII flows.

articles.title: After Iran, China has a powerful new oil price weapon
articles.description: China has emerged as the world's first oil "swing importer," significantly reducing its imports by a third to an eight-year low. This move, driven by necessity during the Iran conflict, demonstrates Beijing's ability to stabilize global oil prices and mitigate geopolitical risks, a stark contrast to historical demand patterns.

articles.title: Dollar Dips on Middle East Peace Deal as Markets Stabilize
articles.description: The U.S. dollar slightly weakened as a preliminary peace deal between the U.S. and Iran boosted global markets and eased oil prices. The Japanese yen remained steady following an expected rate hike by the Bank of Japan. Investors closely watch central bank meetings this week for inflation insights.

articles.title: Explainer: The road to a viable flex-fuel market
articles.description: E20 contains 20% ethanol and 80% petrol. E85 contains 80-85% ethanol, with petrol and permitted components making up the balance. The difference is substantial. E85 is not merely a somewhat stronger version of E20
